channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents six power phrases for prompting Claude Code to accelerate development workflows. The techniques draw from practices used by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, with emphasis on structuring projects, scaling processes, and avoiding common mistakes in AI-assisted coding.

item.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology discusses how design systems and context engineering guide AI agents in modern software development. MCP and AI agents work together to ensure consistent, correct application building by bridging design systems and code.

item.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates how to build a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian as a wiki, connected to Claude and NotebookLM. The workflow implements Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki principles with automatic ingestion of meetings, emails, and research via cron jobs and tools like Granola, creating a persistent memory system that scales beyond the agent's base context window.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search presents a comprehensive news segment covering recent AI models and tools including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, Claude Fable, MiniMax M3, and various research projects in video generation, avatars, and speech synthesis. The video reviews multiple emerging AI capabilities across language models, multimodal systems, and creative generation tools.

item.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model called MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed that achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to maximize inference speed.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as signaling the emergence of a permanent underclass. The video discusses concerns around open-source AI and appears critical of the decision.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video reports that Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after only three days of public availability. The commentary raises concerns about whether frontier AI systems will remain accessible to the public or become restricted to government control.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video analyzing competitive shifts in the AI industry as GPT-6 development approaches. The creator argues that OpenAI's dominance is eroding as Microsoft, Apple, and Google deploy alternative models in flagship products, and discusses benchmark performance across competing systems.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin analyzes Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model withdrawal following a US government export-control directive allegedly tied to jailbreak concerns. The video evaluates whether Fable 5 lived up to hype relative to Opus 4.8 based on pre-withdrawal benchmarks and discusses implications for developers building on these models.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model that applies image-generation diffusion techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion. The architecture shifts local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound, theoretically achieving generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs through bidirectional multi-pass refinement.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government issued an export control directive that forced Anthropic to immediately disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers. Better Stack reports this development as breaking news via YouTube.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a novel approach and achieves up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides a flexible ecosystem supporting multiple interfaces including desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for autonomous agent-based development workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js, powered by Gemma 4, that teaches developers vibe coding and agentic behaviors. It demonstrates local and cloud model serving options including Transformers.js, Ollama, LM Studio, Gemini API, and Google Cloud.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej presents a tutorial on Pi Agent, a framework for building coding agents. The course covers practical implementation with support for real coding agent development.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5's website design generation capabilities through progressive tests, comparing it against Opus 4.8 and showing its ability to replicate design patterns from reference sites. The video argues Fable 5 represents a significant shift in how AI handles web design tasks, with discussion of token efficiency tradeoffs.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service designed for focus and productivity during thinking and building work. The playlists are made and curated by musicians.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service created by Anthropic for thinking and building work. The content is curated by musicians to support focused creative and technical tasks.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that generates full marketing campaigns from a single idea, producing TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts with consistency across formats. The tool automates visual content creation and replaces manual shoot-edit workflows, positioning itself as a competitive advantage for marketing automation in 2026.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini, an AI assistant, to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. The tool helps developers migrate database schemas and explains PostgreSQL concepts to improve their skills in the process.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a framework that enables developers to integrate AI agents directly into React and Next.js applications by rendering real UI components and sharing state, moving beyond bolted-on chatbot windows. The tool supports human-in-the-loop approval workflows and generative UI patterns.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery shares a technique for implementing persistent memory in AI agents. The video demonstrates how to enable agents to retain and recall information across interactions.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform enables unified data visibility across finance, supply chain, HR, and sales functions. She argues that enterprise-scale planning spreadsheets fail at scale, advocates for building complete platforms rather than single features, and emphasizes hiring discipline and trusted customer validation as keys to competing against established players.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: A Mixture of Experts podcast episode hosted by IBM Technology debates whether Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 justifies the hype, analyzing its tier routing system as a Mythos-class model. The panel also discusses Apple's shift away from on-device AI to partner with NVIDIA for cloud compute at WWDC 2026, and whether AI models can detect sarcasm using multimodal inputs or context alone.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic held an opening keynote at Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo featuring engineering and product leaders. The event provided updates on company developments and strategic direction.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false positives, and limiting AI-related work through unclear guardrails. The controversy centers on trust: users question whether they receive the full model, a downgraded fallback, or a weakened version modified behind the scenes. The piece argues that frontier AI systems are increasingly judged by user trust rather than capability alone.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, VP of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research that moves breakthroughs into real-world applications. He covers flood forecasting, MedGemma, Gemini for Science, and tools like Co-Scientist and ERA for scientific discovery. The conversation also touches on speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum milestones including the Willow chip.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech explains how to build AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol to ground agents in enterprise data via Looker. The tutorial covers MCP configuration, the MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml setup, API credential management, and creating agents that translate prompts into data requests.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a ZeroEdit video production pipeline using Claude Fable 5 Code to automatically edit YouTube videos. The system deconstructs successful channels, writes scripts, records voiceover, and uses Claude as an editing engine integrated with Higsfield CLI for full video assembly at approximately $2 per video.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without SQL knowledge. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling and coordinating large numbers of coding agents with Baseten leaders. The conversation covers running agents at scale, agent-to-agent communication and review, and shifting from task parallelization toward system-level design.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses why Meta believes smart glasses could become the next major computing platform for AI assistants. The conversation covers potential use cases like meetings, reminders, and fitness tracking, on-device AI, competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition in wearable devices.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

item.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
item.description: AI Engineer: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that replaces DOM parsing and screenshot-based navigation with structured, named tools that agents call directly. It offers both declarative HTML attribute APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom agent-callable actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

item.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
item.description: Code With Aarohi: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common architecture. MCP uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports both local and remote connections via STDIO or HTTP transports.

item.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Zack Proser describes a four-layer system for asynchronous AI-assisted development that keeps humans available while agents work autonomously. The stack includes signal agents for context management, verification gates for safety, self-improvement loops via conversation history analysis, and holistic monitoring like sleep tracking to prevent burnout.

item.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
item.description: Julia Turc: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

item.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology discusses how AI accelerates the detection of zero-day vulnerabilities and reshapes cybersecurity defense strategies. The video argues that AI-driven vulnerability scanning combined with DevSecOps practices could improve system security despite the risks of AI capabilities.

item.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI skill used to prevent code refactors. The full video is available at the provided link.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three variants: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer, latent mixture of experts, and MTP in their architecture to differentiate from other open-source models.

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Opendoor's exit from India is prompting broader discussion about the relationship between AI capabilities and outsourcing practices. India's emergence as the world's largest Global Capability Center market frames this decision within larger workforce and economic trends.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has only one direct report, presented as evidence of his leadership approach or effectiveness.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella articulated a position that "Everyone Is a Stakeholder" in artificial intelligence. This statement reflects a governance perspective on inclusive participation in AI development and deployment.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic reversed a controversial policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks without user notification. The company acknowledged making the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, and committed to making such safeguards visible going forward.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
item.description: hyhmrright: A user on Anthropic's $200 subscription plan reports that using the Fable model consumed 45% of their weekly quota in a single day for problem-solving and code review, compared to only 80% total usage per week with Opus 4.8. The post criticizes Fable's pricing efficiency relative to user quotas.
